source.title: Amygdala neurocircuitry at the interface between emotional regulation and narcolepsy with cataplexy.

confidence_rationale: Recent (2023) review consolidating CeA / BLA / BNST contributions to cataplexy with explicit framing of emotion -> motor-coupling failure under orexin loss. Lower than Burgess because it is a review rather than primary data, but the mapping to MECH-281's specific claim (BLA/CeA as a canonical downstream target of override_signal) is unusually clean -- this is the framework Sardar et al. propose.
failure_signatures: If CeA, BLA, and BNST contributions to cataplexy are dissociable (e.g., CeA-only lesion replicates the full effect), then MECH-281 may be over-specified by naming the entire amygdala complex; the relevant gain target would be narrower.
The Sardar review notes residual gaps: human functional imaging in cataplexy is sparse, so primate-level confirmation of the rodent circuit remains pending.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The amygdala network -- including the central nucleus (CeA), basolateral amygdala (BLA), and extended amygdala (bed nucleus of the stria terminalis, BNST) -- is a critical node linking emotional regulation to cataplexy in narcolepsy; orexin loss disrupts the normal amygdala -> brainstem coupling that gates emotional arousal into motor output.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-281 names BLA/CeA arbitration as a canonical downstream target of orexin-analog gain. Sardar et al. extend this to include BNST (extended amygdala) as part of the same circuit family. For REE, this suggests the override_signal target is not a single nucleus but a small distributed subset of amygdala-family nuclei whose collective output gates motor recruitment. The framework is consistent with MECH-281 and potentially extends it.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a review, not primary data -- it consolidates existing rodent work and a small set of human imaging studies. The specific prediction MECH-281 makes (override_signal scales the weight of amygdala arbitration in commit-gate threshold computations) is consistent with but not directly tested by the review. Treat as supportive context rather than confirmatory evidence.
mapping.source_context: Review of rodent cataplexy literature plus human functional neuroimaging in narcolepsy with cataplexy.

confidence_components.notes: Frontiers in Neuroscience review, Stanford sleep group (Giardino). The mapping is high because the review explicitly frames amygdala as the emotional-regulation -> motor-coupling node MECH-281 names; source quality is moderate because reviews aggregate but do not generate evidence.
